ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: ni ehã dā̃wolo̱i anukpa le̱ yaye enukpa le̱ ekoṅ, ni eṅõ kpulu le̱ ewo Farao deṅ;
translated: And the Pharaoh's heart became hardened, and he did not listen to them;

ref: and I promise to lead them out of their troubles. I will give them a land rich with milk and honey, where the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites now live.
text: ni mikē̱ ake̱: Madšienye ke̱-dše Mizraim amanehũlũ le̱ mli ke-ya Kanaanbii ke̱ Hitbii ke̱ Amorbii ke̱ Perizbii ke̱ Hivbii ke̱ Iebusbii le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱, šikpoṅ ní fufo̱ ke̱ wò hoo ye̱ no̱ le̱.
translated: And they went out from Egypt, and they came to the land of Canaan, and to the Hittites, and to the Amorites, and to the Perizzites, and to the Hivites, and to the Jebusites, to the place where they were going, and they went and lived there.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Išmaelbii le̱ agbe̱ii ni aṅõ-tšē̱o̱ ame̱-sešibii le̱ ne̱: Išmael kromo̱bi le̱ Nebaiot, ke̱ Kedar ke̱ Adbeel ke̱ Mibsam,
translated: Ishmael had twelve sons: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, and Mibsam

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Ni no se̱ le̱ efo̱ biyō, ni ewole̱ Dina.
translated: You will not see the light of day, you will die, Dina.

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱ yō le̱ ake̱: Mẽba ofe ene̱? Ni yō le̱ kē̱: Onũfũ le̱ lakami, ni miye.
translated: Then Jehovah said to him: What is your name? And he said to him: My name is Onufū, I am a servant.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: Adam said: "I have seen that the one who is good is the one who is kind to others, and the one who is bad is the one who is unkind to others; so I will be kind to others."

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.
translated: Abram was a man of great wealth, he had many cattle, many sheep, many donkeys, many servants, and many possessions.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: My name is: What is your name? I say: My name is Betuel, Nahor is my father and Milka is my mother. I have no brothers, I have no sisters.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: Joseph was sold into slavery by his brothers, and they cast him into a pit. Then Joseph was taken up and sold to the Ishmaelites.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: Jacob said to his sons: "Iosef is here, and Simeon is here, and your brother Benyamin is here with him; may you be well!"

ref: The following men were the heads of their ancestral clans: The sons of Reuben, Jacob's oldest son, were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text: Mene̱mei dši onukpai ye̱ ame̱tše̱mei awei le̱: Israel kromo̱bi Ruben le̱ bii ne̱: Hanok ke̱ Palu, Hezron ke̱ Karmi; mene̱mei dši Ruben wekui le̱.
translated: These are the sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Karmi. These are the sons of Reuben.

ref: We even returned the money we found in our grain sacks when we got back to Canaan. So why would we want to steal any silver or gold from your master's house?
text: Na, šika le̱ ni wo̱na dſere no̱ ye̱ wo̱kotokui le̱ amli le̱ wo̱ke̱-dše̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ tõ *wo̱bahão; té aafe tẽṅ mo̱ ni wo̱o̱dšu dſie̱te̱i lo šika ye̱ onuṅtšo̱ we̱ṅ? *
translated: And, I will tell you the truth, I have not seen any of them, I have not seen them, I have not seen them, the Canaanites, I have not seen them; but tell me, how many of them are there?

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: ni oke̱le̱ ayaht ši gbĩĩ flō ko ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni onyemi le̱ mlila le̱ aadše emli,
translated: I am going to the market to buy some food, because I am hungry.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: *Gbo̱ dšimi, ni nye mibato; nyehãami fũhe ko ye̱ nyeteṅ, koni mato migbonyo ni kã mihīe ne̱.
translated: I am going to the market, I will buy things; I will go to the farm to harvest, and I will bring food home.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ fe šikpoṅ koloi ye̱ ame̱heno̱, ke̱ šĩa koloi ye ame̱heno̱, ke̱ nii fẽ ní wamoo̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ ye̱ ame̱heno̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.
translated: If you are a man who is strong, then you are a man who is strong, and you will be a man who is strong. If you are a man who is strong, then you are a man who is strong.
